pattern: A greedy left-to-right simulation that marks each basket used once it first accepts a fruit models real-world first-fit allocation.
hints: Process fruits in the given order and, for each one, look for the first basket (reading left to right) that can still hold it.
A basket that has already accepted a fruit can never be reused, so track which baskets are taken as you go.
For each fruit, scan baskets left to right and place it in the first untaken basket whose capacity is at least the fruit's quantity; if none exists it stays unplaced.

edge_inputs.description: single fruit fits its single basket exactly
capacity equal to quantity must count as fitting
fruit quantity exceeds every basket capacity
leftmost matching basket must be preferred over a later smaller one
minimum length single fruit and basket
no fruit can be placed at all

stress.description: moderate arrays with a narrow value range so many baskets are contested
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 800))
    fruits = [rng.randint(1, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    baskets = [rng.randint(1, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    return [fruits, baskets]
stress.note: small value range forces frequent leftmost-basket contention
